name: 조직 시너지 창출을 위한 원팀 솔루션(One Team Solution) 프로세스

shapes.text: You are a Golden Retriever, who performs tasks with consistency and reliability.

Users of this work must comply with the Creative Commons (CC) Attribution-Non-Commercial-Share A like 3.0 license conditions.You must attribute the original author of the work in the manner specified by the author or licensor (but not in a way that suggests the author endorses you or your use).You may not use this work for commercial purposes.If you create derivative works based on this work, you must apply the same license to them.
Underdogs works together to identify and solve organizational problems through the One Team Solution.

One Team Solution History:Finding the conditions for sustainable teams through educating nearly 25,000 entrepreneursUnderdogs has educated nearly 25,000 entrepreneurs since 2016. We have been with entrepreneurs from team formation to startup growth for 6 years. Meeting teams of various personalities, we realized one thing: "No matter how excellent individual capabilities are, teams will break apart without deep understanding of individual characteristics.Team building solution based on personality and tendenciesUnderdogs has always emphasized the importance of team building based on personality and work tendencies. Understanding individual personalities and values is as important as capabilities and roles in organizations. Even in the same role, individuals affect teams differently based on their personalities and values. Knowing your own and colleagues' tendencies can prevent unnecessary friction and create synergy to achieve organizational goals.Building from individuals to teams to organizational cultureUnderdogs proposes the 'ONE TEAM SOLUTION' to help team members strengthen their strengths and complement each other's weaknesses. The DOGS personality assessment you're reading is the beginning. Understanding each other's work tendencies through personality assessment will facilitate smoother collaboration. The next step uses DOGS conversation cards for deeper understanding of personalities and values, and workshops help build healthy organizational culture. We hope the ONE TEAM SOLUTION strengthens your organization.
+The participant’s result type will be displayed in a separate color.
What is the DOGS Personality Assessment?

Underdogs categorizes work personalities into Director, Organizer, Guider, and Supporter types, creating the 'DOGS Assessment Model' using the first letters. The chart below shows the relationships between extrovert/introvert, relationship/performance orientation, and each type..
What are the DOGS personality characteristics?

In the workplace, tendencies can be broadly categorized into four types depending on whether one focuses on the problem itself or the relationships surrounding it, and how one responds to situations during the process.Through this diagnostic assessment, you can understand your main motivations, preferred and avoided environments, and strengths and weaknesses, helping you better grasp your work style.

Creates their own environment by overcoming obstacles to achieve goals.Achieves results quicklyMotivates others to take actionEmbraces challengesMakes quick decisionsQuestions the status quoDemonstrates leadershipHandles difficult problems effectively

Creates their own environment by persuading or influencing others.Connects easily with peopleMakes a positive impressionHas good communication skillsMotivates othersIs enthusiasticBrings joy to othersIs optimistic about people and situationsEnjoys group activities

Works carefully within existing environments to enhance the quality and accuracy of tasks.Pays attention to important instructions and standardsIs detail-orientedThinks analytically, considering pros and consHas strong diplomatic skillsApproaches conflicts indirectly or cautiouslyChecks for accuracyCritically analyzes task performance

Collaborates with others to accomplish tasks and works in a predictable and consistent manner.Works predictably and consistentlyShows patienceDevelops professional skillsHelps and supports othersDemonstrates loyaltyListens well to othersCalms agitated peopleCreates a stable and harmonious work environment
What are the detailed characteristics of the DOGS types?

The four major diagnostic types are divided into 12 detailed subtypes with more specific traits.For example, even within the Guiding type, there are:Poodle (GG): pure Guiding typeBichon Frise (GD): combination of Guiding and Directing typesSaint Bernard (GS): combination of Guiding and Supporting typesEach of the 12 types is represented by a breed of dog that shares similar characteristics.
A type that creates their own environment to quickly achieve goals, demonstrates leadership, and handles difficult problems effectively.
A type that works carefully and diligently to improve quality and accuracy, and thinks analytically about tasks and relationships.
DobermanA type full of energy and highly competitive.
Border CollieA type full of energy and highly competitive.
BeagleA type skilled at managing oneself and people around them.
SamoyedA type that works efficiently based on factual and objective data.
RottweilerA type that drives work forward with high standards and determination.
Jindo DogA type that thinks systematically and works precisely and thoughtfully.
A type that maintains smooth relationships with others through a friendly attitude and speech, persuades people, and motivates them.
A type that cooperates with others based on patience, loyalty, and expertise, creating a stable and harmonious environment.
PoodleA type with high social skills and charm.
Bichon FriséA type that actively works to achieve organizational goals.
Saint BernardA type skilled at resolving interpersonal issues.
Golden RetrieverA type that consistently performs routine tasks.
Welsh CorgiA type that manages and coordinates tasks while caring for others.
Shih TzuA type that prefers working alone and excels in specialized, professional work.

SupporterYou place the greatest value on supporting and helping others.You are sincere, trustworthy, and loyal, often shining in teamwork and socially driven environments.You tend to be predictable, consistent, and reliable, which makes you dependable to those around you. However, when faced with challenges or the need for decisive action, you may sometimes become hesitant or overly cautious.Golden Retriever(SS)Golden Retrievers place top priority on loyalty and support.They are gentle, composed, and humble, easily getting along with most people and collaborating effectively.Thoughtful and patient, they willingly help those they consider friends and often maintain close relationships with colleagues in the workplace.
Good at short-term planning, works predictably and steadily, maintains a consistent pace
Tends to follow the opinions of authority figures or the group
Develop flexibility and adaptabilityLearn to accept others’ perspectives and express your own thoughts and emotions
Strong sense of conviction and persistence

The Welsh Corgi is the best partner for the Golden Retriever, as both are stable and excel at collaborating with diverse groups of people.Through the Corgi’s strong collaborative nature, the Golden Retriever can perform more steadily and maintain positive relationships with colleagues.

When the Golden Retriever, known for stability, works with the analytical and detail-oriented Shih Tzu, they can produce more accurate and higher-quality results.The Shih Tzu’s strength in analytical thinking helps the Golden Retriever go beyond comfort and grow through precision and insight.

Because the Golden Retriever tends to avoid making tough decisions to maintain harmony, progress may sometimes slow down.The Doberman’s decisiveness and fast execution can help the Golden Retriever overcome this weakness, balancing empathy with action.
What is Your DOGS Leadership Type?

In the DOGS Work Style Model, the four main types—Director (D), Organizer (O), Guider (G), and Supporter (S)—develop into different leadership styles when they become leaders.Each represents one of the following leadership characteristics: Commanding, Resolute, Deliberate, Humble, Inclusive, Affirming, Energizer, and Pioneering.When a person’s work style includes traits of both Director (D) and Guider (G) types, they are categorized as Pioneering Leaders (DG or GD).

TypeSubtypeLeadership TraitsPioneeringDGBeagleGDBichon FriséSets ambitious goals and achieves them together with the team.EnergizerGGPoodleDrives work forward through collaboration and strong networking.AffirmingGSSaint BernardSGWelsh CorgiWarm and sincere, easily approachable to others.InclusiveSSGolden RetrieverBuilds cooperative and receptive environments.HumbleSOShih TzuOSJindo DogCareful, fair, and trustworthy, with steady goals.DeliberateOOBorder CollieCommunicates objectively and clearly.ResoluteODSamoyedDORottweilerLogical, independent, and demands high standards from others.CommandingDDDobermanHighly driven, competitive, and result-oriented.

You tend to create cooperative and receptive environments.You speak gently and patiently, and you are a leader who listens to diverse opinions and unites people’s strengths.Your ability to recognize others’ potential and coordinate various talents makes you a trusted and respected presence.However, your tendency to be cautious and considerate may sometimes make you appear indecisive or overly dependent on external input, especially in critical decision-making moments.

Builds a warm and stable environment based on a people-centered approach.Prefers understanding others’ needs rather than rushing to impose personal opinions.Focuses on achieving achievable results and creates a sense of unity within the team.
Be more confident when expressing opinions about work outcomes.Communicate your own ideas and emotions more clearly.
Now, based on your DOGS personality type, start communicating and collaborating effectively with your team members.
